europe.head_desc: Learn the 44 countries of Europe and their capitals. Geographic facts, Fun Facts and quiz links.
europe.og_desc: Complete guide to the 44 countries of Europe with capitals and geographic facts.
europe.intro: Europe consists of 44 sovereign states with a total population of about 748 million people and an area of 10.5 million km².
europe.geo_p1: Europe is the second smallest continent but the third most densely populated in the world. It stretches from the Atlantic Ocean in the west to the Ural Mountains in the east, and from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Mediterranean Sea in the south.
europe.geo_p2: The major mountain ranges are the Alps (highest peak Mont Blanc at 4,808 m), the Pyrenees between Spain and France, the Carpathians in Central Europe, and the Caucasus on the southeastern border.
europe.geo_p3: The major rivers are the Volga (longest in Europe), the Rhine, the Danube (crossing 10 countries) and the Thames. Europe is home to some of the oldest and largest cities in the world: London, Paris, Rome and Moscow.
facts: The Vatican (within Rome) is the smallest country in the world with an area of just 0.44 km².
Russia is the largest country in the world — its European part makes up about 40% of the continent.
The European Union has 27 member states and represents one of the largest economies in the world.
Norway, Iceland, Switzerland and Liechtenstein are not EU members but are closely linked to it.
The most populous city in Europe (outside Moscow) is London with over 9 million people.
europe.quiz_subtitle: Do you know all European countries on the map?

africa.head_desc: Learn the 53 countries of Africa and their capitals. Geographic facts, Fun Facts and quiz links.
africa.og_desc: Complete guide to the 53 countries of Africa with capitals and geographic facts.
africa.intro: Africa is the second largest continent and has the most countries in the world — 54 in total. Area: 30.3 million km², population: about 1.4 billion.
africa.geo_p1: Africa stretches from the Mediterranean Sea in the north to the Cape of Good Hope in the south. It is divided into five subregions: North Africa (Maghreb + Egypt), West Africa, Central Africa, East Africa and Southern Africa.
africa.geo_p2: The world's largest desert, the Sahara (9.2 million km²), covers most of North Africa. The Nile, the world's longest river (6,650 km), rises in central Africa and flows into the Mediterranean. The Congo is the world's second largest river by discharge.
africa.geo_p3: Africa's highest mountain is Kilimanjaro in Tanzania (5,895 m). The Great Rift Valley crosses East Africa from north to south, hosting some of the world's largest lakes.
facts: Africa is the fastest-growing continent by population — it is expected to reach 2.5 billion by 2050.
Nigeria is Africa's most populous country with over 220 million people.
Algeria is the largest country in Africa and the 10th largest in the world.
South Africa has 3 official capitals: Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative) and Bloemfontein (judicial).
Ethiopia is one of the few African countries that was never colonised by a European power.
africa.quiz_subtitle: Do you know where every African country is on the map?

asia.head_desc: Learn the countries of Asia and their capitals. The largest continent — from Japan to Turkey. Geography, facts and quiz.
asia.og_desc: Complete guide to the countries of Asia with capitals and geographic facts.
asia.intro: Asia is the largest continent in the world, both in area (44.5 million km²) and population (4.7 billion — over 60% of the world's population).
asia.geo_p1: Asia stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Indian Ocean in the south, and from the Ural River in the west to the Pacific Ocean in the east. It borders Europe to the west (together forming Eurasia) and Africa via the Suez Canal.
asia.geo_p2: Asia is home to the world's highest peak, Mount Everest (8,849 m) in the Himalayas, and also the lowest point on land, the Dead Sea (−430 m). The world's great rivers — the Ganges, Indus, Yangtze and Mekong — all originate here.
asia.geo_p3: Asia is divided into five subregions: East Asia (China, Japan, Korea), Southeast Asia (Thailand, Vietnam, etc.), South Asia (India, Pakistan), Central Asia and West Asia / the Middle East.
facts: China and India are the two most populous countries in the world — together they are home to almost 3 billion people.
Russia, though mainly a European country, has the largest Asian territory — Siberia.
Japan consists of about 6,852 islands, of which the four main ones are Honshu, Hokkaido, Kyushu and Shikoku.
Kazakhstan is the largest landlocked country in the world.
Saudi Arabia has no permanent rivers — the country relies entirely on groundwater and desalination.
asia.quiz_subtitle: Do you know where every Asian country is on the map?

north_america.head_desc: Learn the countries of North America and their capitals. From Canada to Panama — geography, facts and quiz.
north_america.og_desc: Geography guide to the countries of North America with capitals and facts.
north_america.intro: North America stretches from the Arctic Ocean in the north to Panama in the south. It includes 23 sovereign states with a total area of 24.7 million km² and a population of about 600 million.
north_america.geo_p1: North America is geographically divided into three main areas: Canada and the USA (Anglophone North America), Mexico (Hispanic North America) and Central America with the Caribbean islands.
north_america.geo_p2: The Rocky Mountains in the west and the Appalachians in the east are the main mountain ranges. The Mississippi–Missouri is the continent's largest river system. The Great Lakes on the US–Canada border form the world's largest collection of fresh water.
north_america.geo_p3: Central America consists of 7 small countries between Mexico and Colombia. The Caribbean hosts dozens of islands, of which Cuba, Haiti/Dominican Republic, Jamaica and Puerto Rico are the largest.
facts: Canada is the second largest country in the world by area but has a smaller population than California.
Mexico City is one of the largest metropolitan areas in the world with over 21 million people.
Costa Rica has had no army since 1948 — one of the few countries in the world without armed forces.
The Panama Canal, connecting the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans, opened in 1914 and is one of the world's most important shipping routes.
Haiti was the first country in the world to gain independence after a slave revolt, in 1804.
north_america.quiz_subtitle: Do you know where every North American country is on the map?

south_america.head_desc: Learn the 12 countries of South America and their capitals. Amazon, Andes and the world's most impressive landscapes.
south_america.og_desc: Complete guide to the 12 countries of South America with capitals and geographic facts.
south_america.intro: South America consists of 12 sovereign states with a total area of 17.8 million km² and a population of about 440 million. It is the fourth largest continent by area.
south_america.geo_p1: South America is connected to North America via Panama. Along its western edge run the Andes, the world's longest mountain range (7,000 km), with the highest peak Aconcagua in Argentina (6,961 m) — the highest point outside Asia.
south_america.geo_p2: The Amazon, the world's largest river by water volume, flows from the Andes to the Atlantic Ocean across Brazil. The Amazon rainforest — 'the lungs of the Earth' — covers about 5.5 million km².
south_america.geo_p3: In the south, Patagonia is one of the most remote and sparsely populated regions in the world. Lake Titicaca, on the Peru–Bolivia border, is the world's highest navigable lake (3,812 m).
south_america.bolivia_note: * Bolivia has two capitals: Sucre (constitutional) and La Paz (administrative).
facts: Brazil is the largest country in South America and the 5th largest in the world — it occupies almost half the continent.
Argentina has the highest per capita beef consumption in the world.
Chile is the world's longest and narrowest country: 4,300 km long but only 350 km wide on average.
Buenos Aires, Argentina, is known as 'the Paris of South America' for its European architecture and culture.
Peru is home to Machu Picchu, an ancient Inca city and UNESCO World Heritage Site rediscovered by Westerners in 1911.
south_america.quiz_subtitle: Do you know where every South American country is on the map?

greece.head_title: Geography of Greece — Regions, Prefectures & Cities | GeoMix
greece.head_desc: Learn the 13 regions and 74 prefectures of Greece. Capitals, geographic features and major cities.
greece.og_title: Geography of Greece — Regions & Prefectures | GeoMix
greece.og_desc: Guide to the 13 regions and 74 prefectures of Greece with capitals and geographic facts.
greece.intro: Greece is a country in southeastern Europe with an area of 131,957 km² and a population of about 10.7 million. The country is divided into 13 administrative regions and 74 prefectures.
greece.geo_p1: Greece is located at the southern tip of the Balkan Peninsula. It borders Bulgaria and Turkey to the northeast, North Macedonia to the north, and Albania to the northwest. It is washed by three seas: the Aegean Sea, the Ionian Sea and the Mediterranean Sea.
greece.geo_p2: A defining feature of Greek geography is its islands: the country has more than 6,000 islands, of which about 227 are inhabited. The largest islands are Crete, Euboea, Lesbos, Rhodes and Chios.
greece.geo_p3: The highest mountain in Greece is Olympus at 2,918 metres, while the longest river is the Aliakmon. Greece's coastline, including the islands, extends to 16,000 km — one of the longest in Europe.
greece.regions_subtitle: Since 2011 (Kallikratis Programme), Greece is divided into 13 regions. Each region has an elected regional governor and regional council.
greece.cities_p1: Athens is the capital and largest city of Greece, with a metropolitan population exceeding 3.5 million. It is one of the oldest continuously inhabited places in the world — its history spans 3,400 years.
greece.cities_p2: Thessaloniki, the second largest city, is the cultural and commercial centre of northern Greece. Other major cities include Patras (third largest), Heraklion (capital of Crete), Larissa (centre of Thessaly) and Ioannina (centre of Epirus).
facts: Greece has the longest coastline in the Mediterranean and the 11th longest in the world.
The Greek alphabet is one of the oldest still in use today — it forms the basis of both the Latin and Cyrillic alphabets.
About 80% of Greece is covered by mountains or hills — it is one of the most mountainous countries in Europe.
Greece hosted the first modern Olympic Games in 1896 in Athens.
Mount Athos (Athos) in Chalkidiki is an autonomous monastic community and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
greece.quiz_subtitle: Now that you've read about Greek geography, are you ready for the quiz?

oceania.head_desc: Learn the countries of Oceania and their capitals. Australia, New Zealand and the island nations of the Pacific.
oceania.og_desc: Complete guide to the countries of Oceania with capitals and geographic facts.
oceania.intro: Oceania includes Australia, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea and dozens of island nations in the Pacific Ocean. Its area reaches 8.5 million km² with a population of about 45 million.
oceania.geo_p1: Oceania is divided into four subregions: Australasia (Australia, New Zealand, Papua New Guinea), Melanesia (Fiji, Solomon Islands, Vanuatu, etc.), Micronesia (Palau, Micronesia, Marshall Islands, etc.) and Polynesia (Samoa, Tonga, French Polynesia, etc.).
oceania.geo_p2: Australia is the largest country in Oceania and the sixth largest in the world (7.7 million km²). It is the only country to occupy an entire continent. Its highest mountain is Kosciuszko (2,228 m).
oceania.geo_p3: New Zealand consists of two main islands (North and South) and is characterised by volcanoes, fjords and diverse landscapes. Papua New Guinea is the second largest island in the world (after Greenland) and is known for its exceptional biodiversity.
oceania.geo_p4: Many island nations in Oceania face serious risk from rising sea levels due to climate change. Countries such as Kiribati and the Marshall Islands have an average elevation of just 2 metres above sea level.
facts: Australia is home to 10 of the world's 15 most venomous snakes, as well as unique animals such as the koala, kangaroo and platypus.
Nauru is the world's smallest island nation (21 km²) and the third smallest country overall.
New Zealand was the first country in the world to grant women the right to vote, in 1893.
Kiribati is the only country in the world that lies in all four hemispheres — northern, southern, eastern and western.
Papua New Guinea is one of the world's most linguistically diverse countries, with over 800 living languages.

antarctica.head_desc: Learn about Antarctica: territorial claims, research stations, geographic records and the Antarctic Treaty.
antarctica.og_desc: The continent without countries — territorial claims, records and the Antarctic Treaty.
antarctica.intro: Antarctica is the southernmost continent and the only one without a permanent human population or state. It is covered by a vast ice sheet and hosts the South Pole. Its area reaches 14 million km² — almost one and a half times the size of Europe.
antarctica.treaty_intro: In 1959, 12 countries signed the Antarctic Treaty in Washington. Today it has 56 parties. Its key principles:
treaty_points: Antarctica is used exclusively for peaceful purposes — military activities are prohibited.
Free scientific research and data exchange among all countries.
Territorial claims are 'frozen' — no new claims are recognised and existing ones are not cancelled.
Prohibition of nuclear tests and disposal of radioactive waste.
antarctica.claims_subtitle: Seven countries claim sectors of Antarctica — the claims of Chile, Argentina and Britain overlap. No claim is recognised by most countries of the world.
antarctica.stations_subtitle: About 70 active stations from ~30 countries operate in Antarctica. In summer (November–February) the population reaches 5,000 people, while in winter it drops to ~1,000.
facts: Antarctica is technically the world's largest desert — it receives less than 200 mm of precipitation per year in its interior.
In 1969, Argentina sent a pregnant woman to Esperanza base — her son, Emilio Palma, is the first person born in Antarctica.
More than 400 subglacial lakes are hidden beneath the ice sheet — the largest is Lake Vostok, at a depth of ~4 km.
If Antarctica's ice sheet melted, global sea levels would rise by ~58 metres.
Norway claims the largest area — Queen Maud Land makes up about 20% of the continent.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1_body: This Privacy Policy describes how GeoMix (geomix.gr) collects, uses and protects your information when you use our platform. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting it in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).
privacy.s2_intro: GeoMix does not require registration and does not collect personal data to use the quizzes. Specifically:
privacy.s2_item1: We do not require an account or email to use the service.
privacy.s2_item2: We do not store quiz results or usage history on a server.
privacy.s2_item3: We do not sell or share personal data with third parties.
privacy.s3_ga_body: We use Google Analytics to understand how visitors use the platform (e.g. number of visitors, most popular quizzes). The data is anonymous and aggregated.
privacy.s3_adsense_body: We may display ads via Google AdSense. Google may use cookies to personalise ads based on your preferences. You can manage these preferences via
privacy.s4_body: If you send us an email via the contact page, we will use your email address solely to respond to your request. We do not use this information for marketing.
privacy.s5_intro: Under the GDPR, you have the following rights:
privacy.s5_access: Access: The right to know what data we hold about you.
privacy.s5_rectification: Rectification: The right to correct inaccurate data.
privacy.s5_erasure: Erasure: The right to have your data deleted.
privacy.s5_objection: Objection: The right to object to the use of your data.
privacy.s5_contact: To exercise any of these rights, contact us at
privacy.s6_body: We use HTTPS to encrypt all communication between your browser and our servers. We do not store sensitive information.
privacy.s7_body: We may update this policy from time to time. Any changes will be published on this page with an updated date.
privacy.s8_body: For questions about the privacy policy, contact us at

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.s1_body: By using the GeoMix platform (geomix.gr), you accept these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, please do not use the service.
terms.s2_body: GeoMix is a free educational platform of interactive geography quizzes. The platform is provided 'as-is' without guarantee of uninterrupted operation.
terms.s3_intro: You agree to use the platform exclusively for lawful and educational purposes. The following are prohibited:
terms.s3_item1: The use of automated tools (bots) to access the service.
terms.s3_item2: Copying or republishing content without permission.
terms.s3_item3: Any action that disrupts the normal operation of the platform.
terms.s4_body: All platform content (code, design, text) belongs to GeoMix and is protected by applicable intellectual property law. Geographic data comes from open sources (Natural Earth, OpenStreetMap).
terms.s5_body: We make every effort to ensure the accuracy of geographic data and quiz answers. However, we do not guarantee absolute accuracy. If you spot an error, please contact us at
terms.s6_body: The platform may display ads via Google AdSense to fund the free service. Ads are subject to Google's terms and policies.
terms.s7_body: GeoMix is not liable for any direct or indirect damage arising from the use or inability to use the platform. The service is provided for educational purposes and is not an official educational source.
terms.s8_body: These Terms of Use are governed by Greek law and the law of the European Union. Any disputes shall be resolved by the courts of Athens.
terms.s9_body: We reserve the right to amend these Terms of Use. Changes will be announced on this page with an updated date.
terms.s10_body: For questions about the Terms of Use, contact us at

about.title: About GeoMix
about.intro: GeoMix is a free, interactive geography platform that helps students, learners and geography enthusiasts learn countries, capitals and flags through games.
about.what_p1: GeoMix is an educational tool that combines interactive maps with geography quizzes. You can test your knowledge of the countries of Europe, Asia, Africa, North and South America — as well as the regions and prefectures of Greece.
about.what_p2: Each quiz has three play modes: the classic quiz (find the country on the map), reverse mode (choose the correct name or flag from 4 options) and Explore mode (click on each country to see information).
about.who_item1: Primary, middle and high school students preparing for geography exams.
about.who_item2: University students in courses that require knowledge of international geography.
about.who_item3: People who follow quiz competitions or simply love geography.
about.who_item4: Teachers who want a tool for classroom activities.
about.how_step1_desc: Select a continent and type: countries, capitals or flags.
about.how_step2_desc: Find the country on the map or choose from 4 options. You get instant feedback.
about.how_step3_desc: See your score and play again to improve.
about.why_p1: Unlike static lists or flashcards, GeoMix uses interactive maps to connect each country with its geographic location. Research shows that visual-spatial learning aids long-term retention.
about.why_p2: GeoMix is completely free, requires no registration and works on any device — desktop, tablet or mobile.

contact.title: Contact
contact.intro: Got a question, suggestion or spotted an error? We'd love to hear from you.
contact.collab_desc: For suggestions, partnerships or anything else.
contact.faq_a1: Yes, GeoMix is completely free and requires no registration or account.
contact.faq_q2: Can I use GeoMix in the classroom?
contact.faq_a2: Absolutely! GeoMix is designed for educational use. Teachers are free to use it for classroom activities.
contact.faq_q3: I found an incorrect answer — what should I do?
contact.faq_a3: Send us an email at support@geomix.gr with the quiz name and question. We'll fix it as soon as possible.
contact.faq_q4: Will you add more countries or continents?
contact.faq_a4: We plan to expand the platform with more quizzes. Send us your suggestions!

consent.message: We use cookies (Google Analytics) to improve your experience. Data is anonymous.
